articles.title: I got the all-clear after an STI test... but it was WRONG, and there were excruciating consequences. Here's how to avoid the same mistake I made
articles.description: Robert Johnson's story exposes a blind spot in modern sexual health testing - one that doctors warn may be helping fuel America's spiraling STI crisis.

articles.title: San Francisco records 1st overdose death caused by new deadly opioid cychlorphine
articles.description: San Francisco Public Health officials are warning about a new synthetic opioid called cychlorphine. "We believe it is more potent than fentanyl. It was found we believe in someone using a counterfeit pill," said Daniel Tsai, director San Francisco Department of Public Health.

articles.title: ‘He needed intensive care and a team of specialists. He got me instead’: an outback doctor on treating patients a long way from help
articles.description: After working in post-conflict zones, Damien Brown thought he was ready for anything. Then he moved to the Northern Territory, where his 20-bed hospital had to serve an area the size of Norway “It can be harder than aid work,” a colleague cautioned me when I first applied to work in the remote Northern Territory. I’d just returned from six months with Médecins Sans Frontières , volunteering in a corner of Africa that was recovering from decades of civil war, so I’d assumed that I’d be ready for anything. “It’s harder,” my colleague explained, herself an experienced aid worker, “because you don’t expect it to be. And because it shouldn’t be.” Continue reading...

articles.title: It costs $230 out-of-pocket to see a dermatologist. Many Australians go without – despite soaring skin cancer rates
articles.description: Exclusive: Report shows cost of first appointment rose $20 in one year, with steeper rises in Western Australia, South Australia and Tasmania Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Patients are increasingly going without medically necessary dermatological care, the head of the Consumers Health Forum said, as new report reveals the rising cost of the specialty in Australia. Dermatology is expensive and getting pricier, with an average first visit now costing an adult patient without concessions $230 out-of-pocket, while follow-up appointments cost almost $190, the report from health directory Cleanbill found. Continue reading...

articles.title: Barry Manilow, 82, makes first public appearance after cancer surgery
articles.description: Barry Manilow was looking healthy and happy in New York City on April 24, months after he underwent lung cancer surgery. See pictures here...

articles.title: Mark Cuban Slams Healthcare Profits, Calls For Shift From Insurers To Consumers
articles.description: Mark Cuban has blasted the health insurance industry, arguing that many big players behave less like risk managers and more like financial entities that profit from gaps in how healthcare contracts get enforced. He has also been pushing a specially designed bank account model that would redirect a typical Affordable Care Act silver plan–sized monthly payment into savings, limited catastrophic protection and routine care. Cuban, on X wrote that "most insurers aren't insurers," describing them as holding companies that exploit capitated arrangements and self-insured employers while hunting for weak oversight across state, federal and commercial systems. The post was shared as a response to a thread from Larry Levitt , that questioned what consumers get for the overhead insurers add while profits remain strong. Most insurers aren't insurers. They are holding companies that arbitrage capitated systems and self insured employers, looking for weaknesses and lack of contract enforcement in state, federal and commercial organizations https://t.co/0IhJEYa3TK — Mark Cuban (@mcuban) April 24, 2026 Mark Cubans Bold Vision For Healthcare In separate X posts earlier in the week, Cuban laid out a structure built around monthly deposits roughly comparable to an ACA silver plan premium. His example used about $2,100 per month for a family of five. Under the framework, about $300 would buy stop-loss coverage with a $30,000 cap, while around $200 would go to a local direct primary care arrangement. The remaining roughly $1,600 would accumulate in a restricted-use account for approved medical spending, similar in function to a ... Full story available on Benzinga.com

articles.title: HealthRX Blood Support Claims Evaluated: The Health RX Blood Pressure Optimizer Formula Under Investigation
articles.description: New York, NY, April 25, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- This article is an informational overview based on provided materials for HealthRX Blood Support and the Health RX Blood Pressure Optimizer formula. The phrasing "claims evaluated" and "under investigation" reflects consumer search behavior and does not indicate independent testing, third-party analysis, or clinical evaluation of the product. All product descriptions, ingredient references, and positioning statements are derived from official product materials and should be reviewed directly for full context. This overview does not constitute medical, health, or dietary advice. If a purchase is made through affiliate links in this article, a commission may be earned at no additional cost to the buyer. This content is presented as a structured overview of brand materials and does not represent an independent review, ranking, or product evaluation. Important: HealthRX Blood Support is a dietary supplement sold through gethealth-rx.com under the Health RX brand. It is a separate, unrelated product from HealthRx.online, which operates as a GLP-1 prescription telehealth service. Any reviews or complaints about HealthRx.online do not apply to this supplement product. If you've been researching blood health supplements in 2026, chances are good you've come across HealthRX Blood Support . Search interest around terms such as "HealthRX Blood Support review," "does Health RX Blood Optimizer work," and "Health RX Blood Pressure formula ingredients" has been growing steadily — and this overview covers what the formula is, what the ingredients look like in published research, and what to know before ordering. HealthRX Blood Support is a multi-function blood health formula targeting blood pressure, blood sugar, cholesterol, and metabolic wellness . In this context, "under investigation" refers to increased consumer interest and ongoing public discussion around the product's positioning — not any formal investigation or independent review. One detail worth noting upfront: certain purchase options include an additional monthly digital publication subscription per the published Terms and Conditions. Full details are in the pricing section below. Readers should review all purchase terms before completing an order. View the current HealthRX Blood Support offer (official Health RX page) Individual results vary. Dietary supplements are not substitutes for balanced nutrition, regular physical activity, or professional medical guidance. Consult a qualified healthcare provider before starting any new supplement, particularly if you are managing blood pressure, blood sugar, or cholesterol, or if you are currently taking prescription medications. What Is HealthRX Blood Support HealthRX Blood Support is a dietary supplement sold through gethealth-rx.com under the Health RX brand. Customer support is available at 866-634-5294 , Monday through Sunday, 8AM–9PM EST. HealthRX Blood Support is a multi-target blood health supplement combining botanical ingredients and nutritional compounds. The formula is positioned to support blood pressure regulation, blood sugar management, LDL reduction, HDL support, and weight management. As required by federal regulation, the product label carries the FDA disclaimer: these statements have not been evaluated by the Food and Drug Administration, and this product is not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any disease. HealthRX Blood Support Formula Claims: What the Label States The term "claims evaluated" reflects how consumers are currently searching for information about HealthRX Blood Support. HealthRX Blood Support is a multi-target formula designed to support several aspects of metabolic health . The product label and marketing materials include phrases such as "reverses insulin resistance" and "clinically proven to lower high blood pressure." These are label-level statements about the formula's positioning — not findings from a completed clinical trial on this specific finished product. That distinction matters when comparing any supplement to published research. The ingredient overview below covers what peer-reviewed literature shows at the individual compound level and why dosing context is relevant when evaluating any multi-ingredient formula. View the current HealthRX Blood Support offer (official Health RX page) HealthRX Blood Support Ingredient Profile: Published Research Context HealthRX Blood Support contains six primary ingredients. Available research applies to individual compounds and does not represent clinical findings for this finished product as a whole. Here is what the published literature shows at the ingredient level. Berberine Extract is among the most studied botanical compounds in the metabolic health space. A meta-analysis in Evidence-Based Complementary and Alternative Medicine reviewed 14 randomized trials and found berberine associated with meaningful effects on fasting blood glucose, post-load glucose, and hemoglobin A1c. Separate research has examined berberine's potential role in LDL reduction. Published studies typically used 900 to 1,500 mg daily as a standalone compound — a dosing detail addressed in the ... articles.title: Chitra Nawbatt, Author and Investor: Fortt Knox Innovation Lab
articles.description: Author Chitra Nawbatt told Jon Fortt at the IIT Summit in Long Beach that her book "The Code Breaker Mindset" helps young workers cut through AI-distorted noise by deciding on data over perception, substance over form. Her equation: written and unwritten rules times voluntary and involuntary pivots, raised to the exponent of serendipity — preparation, timing, opportunity, energy alignment, and informed intuition built on lifelong pattern recognition.

articles.title: How Joey Albert’s cancer battles reminded her to care for her mental health
articles.description: Joey Albert, who now lives a full life after surviving three cancers, said caring for one’s mental and emotional health is like playing a “mind game,” especially since singers are often at risk of health problems due to intense schedules and piled-up workloads. “Sometimes, it’s not just the physical health that we [should] worry about, but also the mental and emotional health. After three cancers, iisipin mo na ito ba pero lalabanan mo ‘yun. Kailangan mong labanan talaga (you need to fight it. You must fight it),” she said in a press conference for her upcoming “FORTYfied: Joey Albert 45” [...]... Keep on reading: How Joey Albert’s cancer battles reminded her to care for her mental health
